The attack reflects a broader strategy by Iran's so-called axis of resistance, a network that includes Iran, Hezbollah in Lebanon, and the Houthis in Yemen. Each group attacks from a different direction. From the north, Hezbollah launches rockets and drones. From the south, the Houthis fire long range missiles, and from the east, Iran carries out direct ballistic missile strikes. The goal stretch Israel's air defenses across multiple fronts. But here's the key detail. While the Houthis claim their missiles struck targets, Israeli defense systems intercepted the projectiles launched from Yevan, preventing any confirmed damage from that southern front. Instead, the destruction seen in Ramat Gan and across central Israel is being linked to direct Iranian missile strikes, including large barges fired in the same time window. Israel's air defenses, including advanced interception systems, have been under intense pressure, facing attacks from multiple directions at once. According to multiple reports, several cluster munition impact sites have been identified in the Hifa area following the latest wave of Iranian missile strikes. These cluster munitions scattered bomblelets across impacted zones, causing damage to roads and multiple vehicles. So far, there are no immediate reports of casualties from these specific impact sites. However, in a separate incident near Hifa, one person has been reported injured. Emergency services say a 79-year-old man was lightly wounded in Kiryat Ata after a cluster munition struck a residential area. According to Israel's Mendavid Adome, the man was hit by stone debris triggered by the shockwave of the blast. Israeli military assessments indicate that the Iranian missile involved was equipped with a cluster bomb warhead. Such warheads are designed to disperse multiple smaller explosives over a wide area, significantly increasing the spread and impact of the attack. The latest strike comes after a lull of nearly 8 hours before Iran launched a fresh ballistic missile attack targeting northern Israel. Earlier overnight, another Iranian missile carrying a similar cluster warhead spread bomblelets across central Israel, causing additional damage as the missile threat intensifies. videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição A fresh wave of coordinated strikes targeted US positions in Iraq. The Islamic resistance of Iraq has claimed responsibility for six precise operations in just 24 hours. A bold escalation aimed directly at American military presence in the region. According to a statement from Sarah Alia aldam, these strikes are part of an ongoing campaign to force the withdrawal of what it calls occupation forces. The attacks appear to be widespread and coordinated. Multiple explosions were reported in air bill, a key hub in the Iraqi Kurdistan region. Local sources say several kamicazi drones were launched at US targets. The drones reportedly struck their intended objectives with precision. At the same time, another attack was reported near Baghdad International Airport. A US military support base in the area was hit by a drone strike. Footage also shows the Islamic resistance in Iraq launching an Alarab missile toward US forces bases, signaling heightened regional tensions. An Iraqi official confirmed the incident but did not provide further details. There is still no official word on casualties. The resistance group has remained consistent in its messaging. It says operations will continue until all foreign troops leave Iraqi soil. This aligns with the broader agenda of what is often described as the axis of resistance, a network of groups opposing US influence across the Middle East. The intensity of these attacks raises serious questions. Is this a new phase of sustained pressure on US forces or the beginning of a wider regional escalation? Washington has yet to respond in detail, but the pattern is clear. Drone warfare is becoming the weapon of choice. Fast, precise, and difficult to counter. As tensions rise across the region, Iraq is once again at the center of a dangerous geopolitical storm. A massive and coordinated assault is shaking the Middle East tonight. Iran has unleashed one of its most intense missile barges yet. And this time, the battlefield stretches far beyond Israel alone. The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps has confirmed the launch of wave 91. This is part of Operation True Promise 4, a rapidly escalating campaign. Heavy ballistic missiles and attack drones are now striking key targets. The primary focus is Israel's major urban centers. Tel Aviv and Hifer are at the heart of this offensive. According to Iranian claims, the impact has been immediate and severe. 5 million people have reportedly rushed into underground bunkers. Air raid sirens have echoed across cities as explosions intensify. The scale of panic reflects the magnitude of the incoming firepower. The situation on the ground is described as chaotic and overwhelming. Emergency responders are struggling to keep pace with the strikes. Successive explosions have created confusion across multiple locations. But this is not a single front war anymore. Iran has made it clear this is a coordinated regional offensive. New fronts have opened, expanding the scope of the conflict dramatically. From the south, Yemen has entered the battlefield. Onsar forces have launched attacks towards southern Israeli territories. Targets reportedly include sensitive areas near Deona. At the same time, Hezbollah has joined the operation. A joint strike was carried out in Tel Aviv's Jaffa region. This marks a rare and dangerous level of coordination. Iran, Hezbollah, and Yemen are now acting in unison. Officials describe it as a unified resistance front. This alignment signals a long prepared and synchronized strategy. According to Israeli broadcaster Khn, the damage is substantial. A powerful Iranian missile with a heavy warhead struck key locations. Several sites have suffered significant destruction. Strategic Israeli air bases have also come under heavy attack. Teln Paul Mahim and Bengurian airport were reportedly struck. These are among Israel's most critical military installations. Urban centers have not been spared either. Tel Aviv, Hifer, AOT, Bashiba, and the Negev region are affected. Missiles and drones have caused widespread damage and casualties. The conflict is no longer confined to Israeli territory. US military assets across the region have also been targeted. This marks a significant widening of the confrontation. Iran claims it fired four gutter missiles at a US aircraft carrier. The USS Abraham Lincoln was reportedly targeted in this wave. There is no independent confirmation of the extent of damage. US bases in Uwait have also been hit. Ahmed al- Jabar and Ali al-Salm bases reportedly took direct strikes. Both installations are considered strategically vital. In Saudi Arabia, the Alhaj base was also targeted. Iran claims precision ballistic missiles caused heavy damage there. This signals a direct challenge to US military presence. The UAE has also been drawn into the conflict. A reported strike hit a gathering of US flight engineers and pilots. The attack allegedly took place near a major American base. Even more significant is the reported destruction of a radar system. The early warning system at Aldafra base was completely wiped out. This system played a key role in detecting aerial threats. Its loss could impact regional defense capabilities and it raises serious concerns about vulnerability in the Gulf. Iran has also made a dramatic claim in the Persian Gulf. It says an advanced Israeli fighter jet was shot down. According to the IRGC, the jet was hit by naval air defenses. It crashed into waters between Hangam and Keshum Islands. There is no independent verification of this claim so far. Meanwhile, the IRGC claimed that dozens of Americans were either killed or injured in its Wave 90 strikes.

videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição A new and dangerous escalation is unfolding in northern Israel as Iran's latest ballistic missile attack intensifies lethal threat on the battlefield. According to multiple reports, several cluster munition impact sites have been identified in the Hifa area following the latest wave of Iranian missile strikes. These cluster munitions scattered bomblelets across impacted zones, causing damage to roads and multiple vehicles. So far, there are no immediate reports of casualties from these specific impact sites. However, in a separate incident near Hifa, one person has been reported injured. Emergency services say a 79-year-old man was lightly wounded in Kiryat Ata after a cluster munition struck a residential area. According to Israel's Mendavid Adome, the man was hit by stone debris triggered by the shockwave of the blast. Israeli military assessments indicate that the Iranian missile involved was equipped with a cluster bomb warhead. Such warheads are designed to disperse multiple smaller explosives over a wide area, significantly increasing the spread and impact of the attack. The latest strike comes after a lull of nearly 8 hours before Iran launched a fresh ballistic missile attack targeting northern Israel. Earlier overnight, another Iranian missile carrying a similar cluster warhead spread bomblelets across central Israel, causing additional damage as the missile threat intensifies. The attacks appear to be widespread and coordinated. Multiple explosions were reported in air bill, a key hub in the Iraqi Kurdistan region. Local sources say several kamicazi drones were launched at US targets. The drones reportedly struck their intended objectives with precision. At the same time, another attack was reported near Baghdad International Airport. A US military support base in the area was hit by a drone strike. Footage also shows the Islamic resistance in Iraq launching an Alarab missile toward US forces bases, signaling heightened regional tensions. An Iraqi official confirmed the incident but did not provide further details. There is still no official word on casualties. The resistance group has remained consistent in its messaging. It says operations will continue until all foreign troops leave Iraqi soil. This aligns with the broader agenda of what is often described as the axis of resistance, a network of groups opposing US influence across the Middle East. The intensity of these attacks raises serious questions. Is this a new phase of sustained pressure on US forces or the beginning of a wider regional escalation? Washington has yet to respond in detail, but the pattern is clear. Drone warfare is becoming the weapon of choice. Fast, precise, and difficult to counter. As tensions rise across the region, Iraq is once again at the center of a dangerous geopolitical storm. A massive and coordinated assault is shaking the Middle East tonight. Iran has unleashed one of its most intense missile barges yet. And this time, the battlefield stretches far beyond Israel alone. The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps has confirmed the launch of wave 91. This is part of Operation True Promise 4, a rapidly escalating campaign. Heavy ballistic missiles and attack drones are now striking key targets. The primary focus is Israel's major urban centers. Tel Aviv and Hifer are at the heart of this offensive. According to Iranian claims, the impact has been immediate and severe. 5 million people have reportedly rushed into underground bunkers. Air raid sirens have echoed across cities as explosions intensify. The scale of panic reflects the magnitude of the incoming firepower. The situation on the ground is described as chaotic and overwhelming. Emergency responders are struggling to keep pace with the strikes. Successive explosions have created confusion across multiple locations. But this is not a single front war anymore. Iran has made it clear this is a coordinated regional offensive. New fronts have opened, expanding the scope of the conflict dramatically. From the south, Yemen has entered the battlefield. Onsar forces have launched attacks towards southern Israeli territories. Targets reportedly include sensitive areas near Deona. At the same time, Hezbollah has joined the operation. A joint strike was carried out in Tel Aviv's Jaffa region. This marks a rare and dangerous level of coordination. Iran, Hezbollah, and Yemen are now acting in unison. Officials describe it as a unified resistance front. This alignment signals a long prepared and synchronized strategy. According to Israeli broadcaster Khn, the damage is substantial. A powerful Iranian missile with a heavy warhead struck key locations. Several sites have suffered significant destruction. Strategic Israeli air bases have also come under heavy attack. Teln Paul Mahim and Bengurian airport were reportedly struck. These are among Israel's most critical military installations. Urban centers have not been spared either. Tel Aviv, Hifer, AOT, Bashiba, and the Negev region are affected. Missiles and drones have caused widespread damage and casualties. The conflict is no longer confined to Israeli territory. US military assets across the region have also been targeted. This marks a significant widening of the confrontation. Iran claims it fired four gutter missiles at a US aircraft carrier. The USS Abraham Lincoln was reportedly targeted in this wave. There is no independent confirmation of the extent of damage. US bases in Uwait have also been hit. Ahmed al- Jabar and Ali al-Salm bases reportedly took direct strikes. Both installations are considered strategically vital. In Saudi Arabia, the Alhaj base was also targeted. Iran claims precision ballistic missiles caused heavy damage there. This signals a direct challenge to US military presence. The UAE has also been drawn into the conflict. A reported strike hit a gathering of US flight engineers and pilots. The attack allegedly took place near a major American base. Even more significant is the reported destruction of a radar system. The early warning system at Aldafra base was completely wiped out. This system played a key role in detecting aerial threats. Its loss could impact regional defense capabilities and it raises serious concerns about vulnerability in the Gulf. Iran has also made a dramatic claim in the Persian Gulf. It says an advanced Israeli fighter jet was shot down. According to the IRGC, the jet was hit by naval air defenses. It crashed into waters between Hangam and Keshum Islands. There is no independent verification of this claim so far. Meanwhile, the IRGC claimed that dozens of Americans were either killed or injured in its Wave 90 strikes.

A ameaça surge em meio a crescentes ataques a infraestruturas críticas em todo o Golfo, sinalizando uma perigosa mudança de foco para o direcionamento a ativos digitais e tecnológicos.

O desenvolvimento ocorre após recentes ataques a instalações de energia e estratégicas na região, aumentando os temores de que centros de IA avançados e data centers possam se tornar a próxima linha de frente neste conflito crescente.
